paragraphs.text: Unity
Unity Software's response to the European Commission's call for evidence on the Digital Fairness Act

Unity Software ("Unity") welcomes the opportunity to contribute to the European Commission's call for evidence on the proposed Digital Fairness Act (DFA). Unity is a global leading platform with $45\%$ of its workforce based in Europe that helps developers create and grow interactive content and experiences across industries - from gaming and automotive to retail and education. Our real-time 3D (RT3D) technology enables them to build, optimize, and scale content across more than 20 platforms, including mobile, PC, console, and extended reality (XR). Developers retain autonomy over how and where they reach audiences, aligning with the EU's emphasis on platform neutrality and user choice.

Unity supports the DFA's goal of strengthening consumer protection against emerging digital risks, including manipulative interface design ("dark patterns"), addictive monetization loops, exploitative personalization, and friction in subscription management.

At the same time, we encourage a balanced regulatory approach that distinguishes technology providers from content creators and recognizes that responsible monetization is essential to the sustainability of Europe's digital and creative economy. In-app purchases, including in-game currencies, enable developers -many of them small and independent -to fund ongoing innovation while maintaining player choice and fair engagement.

In-app purchases are a legitimate and transparent way for developers to offer additional content and sustain their creative work. We encourage the European Commission to distinguish between harmful practices and well-designed, player-centric monetization models that support long-term engagement, developer sustainability, and consumer choice.

section_path: A balanced, innovation-friendly policy framework
paragraphs.text: We propose that the DFA:

section_path: A balanced, innovation-friendly policy framework
paragraphs.text: Distinguish technology providers from content owners to prevent indirect regulation of infrastructure and ensure proportionate responsibility of infrastructure platforms.

section_path: A balanced, innovation-friendly policy framework
paragraphs.text: Encourage transparency and informed choice in monetization, while avoiding blanket bans that could stifle legitimate engagement design.

section_path: A balanced, innovation-friendly policy framework
paragraphs.text: Treat in-game currencies as digital content, consistent with national case law, to avoid inappropriate application of consumer protection or financial services legislation to interactive entertainment.

section_path: A balanced, innovation-friendly policy framework
paragraphs.text: Ensure contract clarity without imposing disproportionate burdens on smaller studios.

section_path: A balanced, innovation-friendly policy framework
paragraphs.text: Promote consistent cross-border enforcement to reduce legal uncertainty for EU businesses.

section_path: A balanced, innovation-friendly policy framework
paragraphs.text: Recognize that manipulative design practices are already prohibited under existing EU consumer law, and position the DFA as a tool to provide practical guidance and best practices instead of prescriptive restrictions, while harmonizing enforcement across Member States.

section_path: Enhancing transparency in monetization and interface design
paragraphs.text: Unity supports disclosure of virtual item probabilities, dynamic in-app pricing, and randomized reward mechanics -provided that requirements remain technology-neutral and developer-friendly.

section_path: Enhancing transparency in monetization and interface design
paragraphs.text: Our platform already includes tools to label probabilities, trigger confirmation prompts, and surface clear UI disclosures. The DFA should prioritize such disclosures to empower players while avoiding rigid design bans that risk penalizing good-faith innovation.

section_path: Enhancing transparency in monetization and interface design
paragraphs.text: Unity recommends that the DFA treat in-game currencies as digital content, consistent with national case law, ensuring proportional treatment under consumer law and avoiding the inappropriate application of financial services legislation to interactive entertainment.

section_path: Enhancing transparency in monetization and interface design
paragraphs.text: Unity Software number in the EU Transparency Register is: REG 645593698602-42.

section_path: Enhancing transparency in monetization and interface design
paragraphs.text: UNITY.COM

section_path: Enhancing transparency in monetization and interface design
paragraphs.text: Unity®

section_path: Enhancing transparency in monetization and interface design
paragraphs.text: Unity Software’s response to the European Commission’s call for evidence on the Digital Fairness Act

section_path: Enhancing transparency in monetization and interface design
paragraphs.text: We also recommend non-binding EU guidance to distinguish functional UX patterns from deceptive manipulation, preserving developer flexibility while promoting consistent standards.

section_path: Enhancing transparency in monetization and interface design
paragraphs.text: Education and awareness are equally critical. Initiatives like the AdWiseOnline campaign are good examples of how the Commission can help foster a safer, more responsible digital and gaming environment. Industry stakeholders, including Unity, can play a role by voluntarily supporting and amplifying these efforts.

section_path: Ensuring fair digital contracts
paragraphs.text: Unity's Grow Solutions - a modular suite of tools and services for monetization and user acquisition - operate on a transparent, revenue-sharing model that supports choice and fairness:

section_path: Ensuring fair digital contracts
paragraphs.text: Developers decide which services (ad networks, mediation, offerwalls, etc.) to activate.

section_path: Ensuring fair digital contracts
paragraphs.text: Unity earns only when our customers succeed.

section_path: Ensuring fair digital contracts
paragraphs.text: We explicitly avoid bundled pricing or forced service use.

section_path: Ensuring fair digital contracts
paragraphs.text: This approach aligns with the DFA's fairness and transparency objectives and demonstrates how market-based incentives can protect consumer interests without heavy-handed intervention.

section_path: Enforcement consistency and simplification
paragraphs.text: To reduce legal fragmentation and inconsistent enforcement across Member States, we support DFA provisions that strengthen coordination - such as CPC Network enhancements and centralized enforcement guidance - to ensure consistent cross-border application.

section_path: Enforcement consistency and simplification
paragraphs.text: Challenges in coordination between authorities and legal frameworks present obstacles for both businesses and consumers alike, leading to uncertainty and hindering informed decision-making.

section_path: Enforcement consistency and simplification
paragraphs.text: We recommend that the DFA prioritize harmonization and coherence across existing frameworks - including the DSA, DMA, GDPR, AI Act, and consumer law - serving as a coordination mechanism rather than an additional regulatory layer.

section_path: Enforcement consistency and simplification
paragraphs.text: While collective redress mechanisms enhance consumer protection, qualified representative actions should remain well-structured and predictable to avoid excessive litigation that could undermine legal clarity and business stability.

section_path: Responsible design as a competitive advantage
paragraphs.text: Unity is committed to helping developers design responsibly by embedding safeguards directly into our tools. Features such as probability labeling, ad filters, and opt-in prompts promote transparency and trust by default.

section_path: Responsible design as a competitive advantage
paragraphs.text: We would welcome the opportunity to collaborate with the European Commission to help define practical, developer-centric standards - building on industry commitments to transparency in optional in-game purchases.

section_path: Responsible design as a competitive advantage
paragraphs.text: Unity Software number in the EU Transparency Register is: REG 645593698602-42.

section_path: Responsible design as a competitive advantage
paragraphs.text: UNITY.COM

section_path: Responsible design as a competitive advantage
paragraphs.text: Unity Software's response to the European Commission's call for evidence on the Digital Fairness Act

section_path: Linking digital skills and trust
paragraphs.text: Encouraging developers to deliver consumer-friendly experiences is not only about compliance but also about fostering a culture of responsibility through education.

section_path: Linking digital skills and trust
paragraphs.text: Unity contributes to the EU's digital skills agenda through our Learn Unity and Unity Elevate programs -offering free, industry-aligned courses in RT3D development, XR, and simulation design.

section_path: Linking digital skills and trust
paragraphs.text: We support over 315,000 European learners annually, helping close the digital and gender skills gap and promoting responsible design practices. These educational efforts directly advance the DFA's long-term objectives by empowering the next generation of creators to prioritize user rights and consumer trust.

section_path: Our commitment to technical support and collaboration
paragraphs.text: Unity has already embedded many of the protections envisioned by the DFA within our platform. We stand ready to:

section_path: Our commitment to technical support and collaboration
paragraphs.text: Provide technical input to the European Commission's impact assessment.

section_path: Our commitment to technical support and collaboration
paragraphs.text: Demonstrate how Unity's default settings and developer options can serve as practical examples of responsible monetization and design transparency.

section_path: Our commitment to technical support and collaboration
paragraphs.text: Share case studies showcasing developer implementations of safeguards and player-centric monetization models.

section_path: Our commitment to technical support and collaboration
paragraphs.text: Unity is broadly aligned with the DFA's consumer protection objectives but believes it is equally important to ensure that responsible monetization is not unintentionally constrained—especially given how central in-app purchases are to developer sustainability and player choice.

section_path: Our commitment to technical support and collaboration
paragraphs.text: We welcome the opportunity to participate in stakeholder consultations, expert workshops, and policy dialogue as work on the DFA advances.

section_path: Our commitment to technical support and collaboration
paragraphs.text: Unity Software number in the EU Transparency Register is: REG 645593698602-42.
